key_insight: HiMuon speeds up Muon-style optimization by replacing one global Newton-Schulz update with independent Newton-Schulz updates on small tiles of each weight matrix.
engineering_relevance: This makes optimizer steps more GPU-friendly and scalable by lowering compute from full-matrix coupling to tiled dense kernels that can be batched, chunked, and scheduled by tile size.
method: Partition each momentum-gradient matrix into T×T tiles.
Apply the same finite Newton-Schulz map separately to each tile.
Reassemble tile outputs, preserving interactions within tiles but not across tile boundaries.
evidence_signal: The paper reports experiments on transformer training and controlled matrix-function diagnostics, showing improved optimizer-step efficiency while remaining close to full-matrix Muon in the tested settings.
watch_item: Because HiMuon is a local tiled map rather than a true approximation of the full-matrix update for finite tile sizes, its behavior may depend strongly on tile size and implementation details.
paper_title: Hierarchical Muon: Tiled Newton-Schulz Updates for Efficient Muon Optimization
